arabic_text: وَالْمُرْسَلَتِ عُرْفًا ﴿١﴾ فَالْعَصِفَتِ عَصْفًا ﴿٢﴾ وَالنَّشِرَتِ نَشْرًا ﴿٣﴾ فَالْفَرِقَتِ فَرْقًا ﴿٤﴾ فَالْمُلْقِيَتِ ذِكْرًا ﴿٥﴾ عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا ﴿٦﴾ إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَوَقِعٌ ﴿٧﴾
english_text:
Don't need God | No purpose to creation | No resurrection
Death & Destruction | Life & Prosperity | Resurrection & Accountability
Indeed (without a doubt) — promise — event
alt_text: Three-column comparison table mapping Quran 77:1-7 (Sura al-Mursalat) onto three denials it answers: 'Don't need God', 'No purpose to creation', 'No resurrection'. Each column pairs an Arabic verse with a thematic Urdu/English label ('Death & Destruction', 'Life & Prosperity', 'Resurrection & Accountability'). The closing verse (إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَوَقِعٌ — 'Indeed what you are promised shall occur') seals all three.
notes: Pedagogical exposition of Sura al-Mursalat (Q77) as a chiastic argument against three denials.
